claims.claim_text: The methodological architecture rests on three interlinked components: (i) a time-weighted effluent quality index that aggregates pollution loads, (ii) an aeration energy cost model proportional to oxygen transfer intensity and reactor geometry, and (iii) a piecewise-linear fine function that converts effluent quality deviations into pollutant-specific monetary penalties.
source_chunk_ids: Stare et al. - 2007 - Comparison of control strategies for nitrogen removal in an activated sludge process in terms of ope_a b s t r a c t_0_18
Machado et al. - 2020 - Systematic comparison framework for selecting the best retrofitting alternative for an existing wate_EDITOR'S CHOICE— RESEARCH ARTICLE_0_81

claims.claim_text: The Benchmark Simulation Model framework employs a dimensionless Effluent Quality Index (EQI) that integrates the weighted concentrations of multiple pollutants over a specified evaluation window.
source_chunk_ids: Stare et al. - 2007 - Comparison of control strategies for nitrogen removal in an activated sludge process in terms of ope_a b s t r a c t_0_18
Machado et al. - 2020 - Systematic comparison framework for selecting the best retrofitting alternative for an existing wate_EDITOR'S CHOICE— RESEARCH ARTICLE_0_81

claims.claim_text: The weighting factors, historically calibrated to the Flanders regional tariff, assign relative monetary weights to total suspended solids, chemical oxygen demand, biological oxygen demand, total Kjeldahl nitrogen, and nitrate-nitrogen.
source_chunk_ids: Machado et al. - 2020 - Systematic comparison framework for selecting the best retrofitting alternative for an existing wate_EDITOR'S CHOICE— RESEARCH ARTICLE_0_80

claims.claim_text: Because the weights are fixed and the aggregation is linear, the marginal cost attributed to any incremental gram of a given pollutant remains constant within each simulation period, a property that is computationally convenient but insensitive to the nonlinear cost escalation real regulatory regimes impose once discharge limits are breached.

claims.claim_text: The BSM energy model expresses aeration energy AE as a function of the volumetric oxygen transfer coefficient and reactor volume.

claims.claim_text: Any DR intervention that reduces K_La,i during a curtailment window directly reduces AE, yielding quantifiable energy savings proportional to both the intensity and the duration of aeration reduction.

claims.claim_text: The piecewise-linear effluent fine model introduced by Stare et al. (2007) decomposes the EQI back into pollutant-specific monetary penalties.

claims.claim_text: The dual-slope Heaviside structure produces a discontinuous jump in marginal cost at the regulatory threshold, creating a strong economic incentive for the plant to remain compliant while still penalizing residual pollution below the limit.

claims.claim_text: The economic equilibrium governing the optimal DR operating point maps onto the classical permit-market clearing condition whereby the marginal abatement cost equals the permit price.
source_chunk_ids: Jin et al. - 2014 - The Regulatory Strategy in Emissions Trading System under Costly Enforcement_Document_0_0

claims.claim_text: In the DR context, the marginal energy savings from an additional unit of aeration reduction must equal the marginal increase in effluent fines at the optimal operating point.
source_chunk_ids: Jin et al. - 2014 - The Regulatory Strategy in Emissions Trading System under Costly Enforcement_Document_0_0

claims.claim_text: The equilibrium condition mirrors the result of Jin et al. (2014), where the firm's marginal abatement cost c_i'(a_i) = p at equilibrium, with p the permit price.
source_chunk_ids: Jin et al. - 2014 - The Regulatory Strategy in Emissions Trading System under Costly Enforcement_Document_0_0

claims.claim_text: In the DR application, the shadow price of effluent quality — the implicit price the facility pays for each additional pollution unit discharged — plays the role of the permit price.
source_chunk_ids: Jin et al. - 2014 - The Regulatory Strategy in Emissions Trading System under Costly Enforcement_Document_0_0

claims.claim_text: As Garibay-Rodriguez et al. (2017) demonstrated through sensitivity analysis of the enforcement multiplier φ, deviations from this equilibrium either waste resources on excessive treatment or incur disproportionate fines.
source_chunk_ids: Garibay-Rodriguez et al. - 2017 - Optimal water management in macroscopic systems under economic penalty scenarios_Sensitivity analysis with respect to parameter /_0_64

claims.claim_text: The shadow price of effluent quality can be bounded using a three-part policy structure analogous to the Roberts–Spence hybrid instrument described in the environmental economics literature.
source_chunk_ids: Xenarios and Bithas - 2012 - The Use of Environmental Policy Instruments for Urban Wastewater Control Evidences from an Internat_Review on Regulatory and Economic Instruments_0_5
Xenarios and Bithas - 2012 - The Use of Environmental Policy Instruments for Urban Wastewater Control Evidences from an Internat_Introduction_0_4

claims.claim_text: Under the three-part policy design, a tax ceiling caps the maximum marginal cost the facility faces for additional discharge, while a subsidy floor establishes a minimum reward for emission reductions beyond the permit allocation, and within the interior band, the shadow price floats at the market-clearing level.
source_chunk_ids: Xenarios and Bithas - 2012 - The Use of Environmental Policy Instruments for Urban Wastewater Control Evidences from an Internat_Review on Regulatory and Economic Instruments_0_5

claims.claim_text: This banding structure hedges against the regulator's uncertainty about true abatement costs and provides upper and lower bounds on the operating cost trajectory across a range of DR intensities.
source_chunk_ids: Xenarios and Bithas - 2012 - The Use of Environmental Policy Instruments for Urban Wastewater Control Evidences from an Internat_Review on Regulatory and Economic Instruments_0_5

claims.claim_text: In the wastewater DR setting, the tax ceiling corresponds to the steeper fine slope Δβ_j triggered upon limit exceedance, while the subsidy floor can be interpreted as the baseline energy cost savings rate attainable under non-DR operation.
source_chunk_ids: Stare et al. - 2007 - Comparison of control strategies for nitrogen removal in an activated sludge process in terms of ope_a b s t r a c t_0_18
Xenarios and Bithas - 2012 - The Use of Environmental Policy Instruments for Urban Wastewater Control Evidences from an Internat_Review on Regulatory and Economic Instruments_0_5

claims.claim_text: The aeration reduction design space is spanned by four parameters: (1) the start time of the DR curtailment event within the diurnal cycle, (2) the duration of aeration reduction, (3) the intensity of K_La reduction relative to the baseline setpoint, and (4) the frequency of curtailment events per evaluation period.
source_chunk_ids: Stare et al. - 2007 - Comparison of control strategies for nitrogen removal in an activated sludge process in terms of ope_a b s t r a c t_0_18

claims.claim_text: Latin Hypercube Sampling (LHS) provides a space-filling experimental design that efficiently covers the four-dimensional domain, avoiding the combinatorial explosion of full-factorial designs while ensuring adequate coverage of parameter interactions.
source_chunk_ids: Stare et al. - 2007 - Comparison of control strategies for nitrogen removal in an activated sludge process in terms of ope_a b s t r a c t_0_18

claims.claim_text: No single environmental policy instrument is considered to be superior along all dimensions, and the assessment of even a single dimension is highly dependent on various conditions.

claims.claim_text: Taxes and permits are claimed to entail less aggregate costs for controlling pollution than standards.

claims.claim_text: The optimal social regulatory strategy to achieve the fixed aggregate emissions target is encouraging the firms to be compliant, with the permit price playing a central role in firms' decision-making.
source_chunk_ids: Jin et al. - 2014 - The Regulatory Strategy in Emissions Trading System under Costly Enforcement_Document_0_0

claims.claim_text: The firms' optimal decision of production and abatement depends only on permit price, independent of monitoring level and penalty function shape.
source_chunk_ids: Jin et al. - 2014 - The Regulatory Strategy in Emissions Trading System under Costly Enforcement_Document_0_0

claims.claim_text: Effluent fines were calculated considering ammonium, total nitrogen, and phosphate as the main pollutants using a cost function with dual slopes (below and above discharge limits) and a Heaviside step function.

claims.claim_text: A well implemented system of economic penalizations is an interesting approach to deal with pollution abatement over water bodies, and with thorough analysis sustainability can be evaluated to design a robust penalization scheme that allows flexibility but does not fall outside environmental goals.

claims.claim_text: The resulting paired response surfaces — energy savings and effluent fines as functions of the four-dimensional aeration parameter vector — provide the empirical basis for locating the equilibrium operating point where marginal energy savings equal marginal fine increases.

claims.claim_text: Aeration energy is calculated using K_La,i (oxygen transfer rate), V_i (reactor volume), and V_ref (reference volume of 1333 m³), following the equation suggested in Copp (2002) and improved by including reactor volumes.
